topic.name: World War II
topic.description: Major events of World War II from 1939-1945, focusing on American involvement.

events.title: Germany Invades Poland
events.description: Nazi Germany invaded Poland, beginning World War II in Europe. Britain and France declared war on Germany two days later.
events.significance: The invasion triggered the start of World War II and demonstrated the effectiveness of German blitzkrieg tactics.

events.title: Attack on Pearl Harbor
events.description: Japan launched a surprise military strike on the U.S. naval base at Pearl Harbor, Hawaii, killing over 2,400 Americans and destroying much of the Pacific Fleet.
events.significance: The attack brought the United States into World War II and unified American public opinion for war.

events.title: U.S. Declares War
events.description: Following President Roosevelt's 'Day of Infamy' speech, Congress declared war on Japan. Germany and Italy then declared war on the United States.
events.significance: American entry into the war transformed the conflict into a truly global war and ultimately tipped the balance toward Allied victory.

events.title: Battle of Midway
events.description: U.S. Navy forces defeated a Japanese attack on Midway Atoll, sinking four Japanese aircraft carriers and turning the tide in the Pacific War.
events.significance: The victory at Midway ended Japanese naval superiority and put the U.S. on the offensive in the Pacific.

events.title: D-Day Invasion
events.description: Allied forces launched the largest amphibious invasion in history on the beaches of Normandy, France, beginning the liberation of Western Europe.
events.significance: D-Day opened a second front in Europe and began the final push to defeat Nazi Germany.

events.title: Battle of the Bulge
events.description: Germany launched a major surprise offensive through the Ardennes forest, creating a 'bulge' in Allied lines before being pushed back.
events.significance: The failed German offensive depleted Nazi resources and hastened the end of the war in Europe.

events.title: Iwo Jima
events.description: U.S. Marines captured the strategic island of Iwo Jima after intense fighting. The iconic flag-raising on Mount Suribachi became a symbol of American sacrifice.
events.significance: The capture of Iwo Jima provided a base for fighter escorts and emergency landings for B-29 bombers attacking Japan.

events.title: Germany Surrenders (V-E Day)
events.description: Nazi Germany surrendered unconditionally to the Allied forces, ending World War II in Europe.
events.significance: V-E Day marked the end of the Nazi regime and allowed the Allies to focus resources on defeating Japan.

events.title: Atomic Bomb Dropped on Hiroshima
events.description: The United States dropped an atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, killing approximately 80,000 people instantly and many more from radiation.
events.significance: The first use of nuclear weapons in warfare demonstrated their devastating power and hastened Japan's surrender.

events.title: Japan Surrenders (V-J Day)
events.description: Japan announced its surrender following the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, ending World War II.
events.significance: Japan's surrender ended World War II and began the American occupation and reconstruction of Japan.
